acceptanceChecks.description: Universal AST envelopes can record lossless source/CST, semantic symbols, effects/control/dataflow, runtime model, and projection/merge evidence as separate layers.
Validation catches broken cross-layer references without making every optional layer mandatory.

items.prompt: Improve the Frontier Lang kernel universal AST envelope so it better models the layered design needed for a universal language: source/CST, semantic index, effect/control/dataflow graph, runtime model, and transform/merge evidence. Keep it backwards compatible and dependency-free. Add tests and declarations.

acceptanceChecks.description: Loss records and source maps can explain exact, declaration-level, estimated, and blocked preservation.
Validation detects dangling loss/evidence/source-map links and span precision problems.

items.prompt: Tighten source preservation and loss accounting in frontier-lang-kernel. Add structured helpers or validation where useful, but keep API small. Tests should prove source map/loss/evidence links are queryable for semantic merge review.

acceptanceChecks.description: Merge candidates expose stable conflict keys for symbols, regions, native spans, source subtrees, effects, and generated output.
Classification distinguishes safe, safe-with-losses, review-required, and blocked candidates with evidence reasons.

items.prompt: Improve semantic merge admission in frontier-lang-kernel. Focus on conflict-key quality, readiness classification, evidence reasons, and tests. Do not auto-merge opaque/dynamic/effectful changes.

acceptanceChecks.description: Patch replay/hash invariants are explicit enough for agents to use as evidence.
Tests cover invalid base hash, invalid target hash, duplicate ids, merge-law overlap, and disjoint safe patches.

items.prompt: Harden patch/replay and merge-law invariants in frontier-lang-kernel. Add focused tests or helper APIs that make replayable state and semantic merging more inspectable. Keep backwards compatibility.
